Cyrtochilum fractum is a type of terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water. The classification of Cyrtochilum fractum is further refined to the subfamily Orchidoideae. intro: Cyrtochilum fractum, also known as the broken Cyrtochilum orchid, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique and distinctive appearance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents found in Cyrtochilum fractum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
